page: Microsoft Azure EC64ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 2458 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 86376.83 and a PassMark CPU score of 62823.72, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory performance is highly competitive, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3058.64 and a small-block read-only memory bandwidth of 4162.65 GB/sec. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds are moderate, measuring 121.12 GB/sec and 62.68 GB/sec respectively. The server also demonstrates strong database performance, achieving 62.79 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 25708.86 in PassMark database operations.

While the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and small-model inference, it exhibits limitations in large-scale AI workloads. For instance, text generation using a large 70B LLM model drops to 2.74 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Consequently, this server is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and data-intensive processing pipelines, rather than large-scale deep learning model execution.
description: The Microsoft Azure EC64ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server includes 2458 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. It delivers top-tier memory performance, as evidenced by a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and strong multi-core processing capabilities in stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks. Database and in-memory operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 62.79 million operations per second. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its efficiency for large-scale LLM text generation. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and enterprise caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC64ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage for demanding database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC64ads_cc_v5 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC processing and local storage for intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
Suited for large databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
